svg.text: Complete scheduling pipeline from trigger to output
B depends on A, B due 3/27 → A ≤ 3/27
Place fixed tasks at exact specified times → locked
Place rigid habits at preferred time (flex if past) → locked
These never move again. Reserve occupancy in day grid.
Each phase processes P1→P2→P3→P4 in order
Place habits early at preferred time windows (by priority)
Secures daily habit slots before flexible tasks claim capacity
Place deadline tasks as late as possible (at/before due date)
Walk dependency chain: deps placed first
Spread control: P3/P4 don't start too far before deadline
P3: earliest = deadline - 50% of deadline window
P4: earliest = deadline - 33% of deadline window
Keeps low-priority work from consuming early capacity
Missed deadlines → place forward from today
These are failures, but still attempt to schedule
Early-place from today forward into available gaps
Tasks with downstream deps: late-place near dependency ceiling
Scan forward from date, find gaps, place at start of gap
Respect: when-windows, location, min chunk, split rules
Scan backward from deadline, find gaps, place at end of gap
Splittable tasks broken across multiple gaps
Check location availability via tool matrix
Swap low-priority early tasks with high-priority later tasks
Push urgent work forward, defer less-urgent. Max 20 swaps.
Move deadline tasks into earlier gaps (compress work)
Respects spread constraint. Restores original if can't fit.
Bump non-habit tasks off day to fit habits
All-or-nothing: rollback if bumped can't refit
Within each day, assign best time slots
Post-greedy refinement — try random moves, keep if score improves
Each move: validate constraints → score → keep if improved, revert if not
Re-score fully every 100 iterations to correct drift
total = Σ (weight × penalty) for all 8 dimensions
Used by hill-climber to evaluate moves and as final quality metric
Sorted by ID to avoid deadlocks
Re-runs if: new day, >30min, past tasks, new tasks
